title: Softer
excerpt: You hold my softness like something to overcome.
body:
You hold my softness like something to overcome.
I am asking, in the only language love has ever given me:
be a little less sword, a little more hand.

title: Trailhead
excerpt: I used to think love was arrival, / a porch light, a hand already waiting.
body:
I used to think love was arrival,
a porch light, a hand already waiting.

Now I think it is walking beside someone
while the pines keep their own counsel,
while the trail narrows, widens, disappears.

You step ahead.
I fall back.
Then we trade places without naming it.

Somewhere in that quiet adjustment,
I learn compatibility is not sameness,
but rhythm.

Not two hearts beating as one,
but two people listening
hard enough
to keep walking.

title: Son Without a Map
excerpt: No one taught me how to be a man.
body:
No one taught me how to be a man.

So I studied rivers,
how they bend without apology,
how they carry what they can
and leave the rest to stone.

I studied my mother's tired hands,
the way silence can become shelter
or a room with no door.

I studied love
and found it was not conquest,
not certainty,
not the loudest voice at the table.

Maybe manhood is smaller than that.

Maybe it is learning
to stay gentle
without becoming absent.

title: Field Notes on Love
excerpt: Love is not the mountain. / It is the person who stops with you halfway up.
body:
Love is not the mountain.

It is the person
who stops with you halfway up
to watch fog lift from the valley.

It is someone asking,
did you eat?
and meaning,
are you still here with me?
